items.title: Who were the pilots killed in the LaGuardia runway collision? | Hanomansing Tonight
items.summary_auto: Family, friends and community members are mourning Antoine Forest and Mackenzie Gunther, the two Air Canada Express pilots killed in Sunday’s crash at New York City's LaGuardia Airport.

items.title: Pilots killed in LaGuardia runway collision mourned by family, community | Hanomansing Tonight
items.summary_auto: Family, friends and community members are mourning Antoine Forest and Mackenzie Gunther, the two Air Canada Express pilots killed in Sunday’s crash at New York City's LaGuardia Airport.

items.title: Minister pressed why just 1 Iranian official deported after 24 deemed part of terror group
items.summary_auto: Public Safety Minister Gary Anandasangaree faced pointed questions Tuesday about why the federal government has deported one Iranian official, despite longstanding concerns about how the regime operates in Canada and abroad.

items.title: Operations suspended at B.C. heli-skiing company after 3 Swiss nationals killed in avalanche
items.summary_auto: The Swiss Consulate General in Vancouver has confirmed the three people killed in Sunday's avalanche in northern B.C. were Swiss nationals. They were part of a group of four that was heli-skiing on Mount Knauss, about 30 kilometres north of Terrace.

items.title: OpenAI shuts down Sora app amid rising concerns about deepfakes and consent
items.summary_auto: OpenAI is shutting down its social media app Sora, which went viral last fall as a place to share short-form videos generated by artificial intelligence, but also raised alarms in Hollywood and beyond about the dangers of letting people create AI videos that resulted in nonconsensual images and realistic deepfakes.

items.title: Pilots killed in LaGuardia runway collision mourned by family, community
items.summary_auto: Antoine Forest, a 30-year-old man from Coteau-du-Lac, Que., was one of two pilots who died in the crash at New York’s LaGuardia airport on Sunday night.

items.title: Hometown of Quebec pilot killed in LaGuardia plane crash feels his loss
items.summary_auto: Antoine Forest, a 30-year-old man from Coteau-du-Lac, Que., was one of two pilots who died in the crash at New York’s LaGuardia airport on Sunday night.

items.title: Pride festivals seek $3M from Ottawa as corporate sponsors pull back amid DEI backlash
items.summary_auto: Pride festivals are seeking $3 million annually from Ottawa to fill a funding gap left by corporations pulling back funding amid a backlash to diversity, equity and inclusion programs.

items.title: Landmark verdict in New Mexico finds Meta violated state child safety laws
items.summary_auto: A New Mexico jury found Tuesday that social media conglomerate Meta is harmful to children's mental health and in violation of state consumer protection law.

items.title: Blue Jays sportscaster Hazel Mae to publish memoir this fall
items.summary_auto: Known for her field-side reporting on the Toronto Blue Jays, Mae wrote the book Before I Let You Go, which is set to release on Nov. 10.

items.title: Montreal-area byelection to use write-in ballots due to dozens of protest candidates
items.summary_auto: The head of Elections Canada said voters will have to write in their pick in next month's byelection in an off-island Montreal suburb due to the long list of protest candidates running in the riding.

items.title: Canada pauses removals of inadmissible individuals to several Middle East countries
items.summary_auto: Canada is temporarily halting removals to several more Middle East countries due to "volatile and unpredictable conditions" caused by the U.S.-Israel war with Iran that has spread to surrounding nations.

items.title: Fact check: Fake CBC articles use this business writer’s byline
items.summary_auto: Jenna Benchetrit's face and name have been used in fake CBC articles intended to scam people into sending money. She’s the senior business writer for CBC News.

items.title: Epic Games to lay off more than 1,000 employees as Fortnite usage drops
items.summary_auto: Epic Games will cut more than 1,000 jobs following a drop in engagement for Fortnite, the latest layoffs in the video game industry where growth has stalled due to economic uncertainty.

items.title: Porter to launch flights from revamped Montreal Metropolitan Airport
items.summary_auto: Porter Airlines plans to launch a dozen routes this summer from the overhauled Montreal Metropolitan Airport, as Canada's third-largest carrier seeks to gain purchase in the competitive domestic market and start putting its half-billion-dollar investment to work.

items.title: Porter to launch flights from revamped Montreal airport
items.summary_auto: Porter Airlines plans to launch a dozen routes this summer from the overhauled Montreal Metropolitan Airport, as Canada's third-largest carrier seeks to gain purchase in the competitive domestic market and start putting its half-billion-dollar investment to work.

items.title: WNBA and players unanimously approve agreement, setting up sprint to May season opener
items.summary_auto: The WNBA board of governors unanimously ratified the terms of a new collective bargaining agreement on Tuesday.

items.title: Upgrades to Toronto Stadium unveiled with World Cup games on horizon
items.summary_auto: Officials revealed Tuesday that all major updates for Toronto Stadium at Exhibition Place are finished in advance of World Cup games this summer.

items.title: The Best of Both Worlds? Miley Cyrus back as Hannah Montana in new anniversary special
items.summary_auto: The anniversary special, which started streaming Tuesday on Disney+ and Hulu, celebrates 20 years since the show's premiere. Filmed in front of a live audience, it features archival footage and is centred on an interview with MileyCyrus — now 33 and a genuine pop star — with podcast host Alex Cooper.

items.title: Israel's defence minister says military plans occupy swath of southern Lebanon
items.summary_auto: Israel will occupy southern Lebanon up to the Litani River to create a "defensive buffer," Defence Minister Israel Katz said on Tuesday, spelling out for the first time Israel's intent to seize territory amounting to nearly a tenth of Lebanon.

items.title: Graduation ceremonies should steer clear of 'divisive or contentious issues,' says Ontario memo to school boards
items.summary_auto: The memo, signed by Education Minister Paul Calandra and addressed to educators and school board staff, warns that failure by school boards to meet those expectations “poses a real risk to student well-being.”

items.title: Hiker dies after slipping down B.C.'s Brunswick Mountain while trying to help companion
items.summary_auto: A man who slipped and fell between 100 and 150 metres while trying to help a companion during a hike on one of the tallest mountains in Metro Vancouver's North Shore has died of his injuries.

items.title: Montreal Victoire captain Marie-Philip Poulin to sit out at least 2 more weeks with leg injury
items.summary_auto: The Montreal Victoire have placed captain Marie-Philip Poulin on long-term injured reserve, the Professional Women's Hockey League team announced Tuesday.

items.title: Justice minister won't meet premiers' demand for 'sea change' to how judges are appointed
items.summary_auto: Justice Minister Sean Fraser says he has no plans to change how judges are appointed in Canada after four premiers wrote to Prime Minister Mark Carney asking for more of a say in the process.

items.title: NASA plans $20B moon base, but pauses lunar space station project
items.summary_auto: NASA is pausing plans to deploy a space station in lunar orbit and will instead use its components to construct ‌a $20 billion base on the moon's surface over the next seven years, its new chief Jared Isaacman said on Tuesday.

items.title: RCMP probe corruption allegations at Calgary city hall; warrants executed on former mayor, councillors
items.summary_auto: An RCMP-led corruption investigation is underway and search warrants were executed on a number of properties, including the homes of former mayor Jyoti Gondek, former councillor Sean Chu and sitting councillor Andre Chabot.

items.title: Man who lost family on Air India Flight 182 honoured with Order of Canada
items.summary_auto: Mahesh Chandra Sharma, a highly respected Concordia University professor known for his philanthropic work, was appointed to the Order of Canada in March. He set up scholarships honouring his family members who were victims of the 1985 Air India bombing.

items.title: Manitoba removing provincial sales tax from more food sold at grocery stores: premier
items.summary_auto: Provincial sales taxes will be removed from more grocery store items under Manitoba's latest budget, and one researcher wonders if other provinces will follow.

items.title: Manitoba to cut PST on all food items at grocery stores in July
items.summary_auto: Provincial sales taxes will be removed from more grocery store items under Manitoba's latest budget, and one researcher wonders if other provinces will follow.

items.title: Federal minimum wage increasing to $18.15 per hour
items.summary_auto: The federal minimum wage will rise by 40 cents next week, a scheduled increase that keeps the wage in line with inflation.

items.title: 'Fragmented, inconsistent, unsafe': Commission calls for sweeping change to sport in Canada
items.summary_auto: A commission studying the future of sport in Canada has called for sweeping change to the way sport is structured, after its work found “the widespread presence of maltreatment and abuse” in a poorly resourced and organized system.

items.title: Record-breaking heat dome growing to cover nearly the entire U.S.
items.summary_auto: A huge heat dome is spreading across the United States. After breaking March records in 14 states with temperatures as high as 44.4 C, it's now widening and may become the most expansive heat dome ever.

items.title: Mysterious posters hinting at Céline Dion's rumoured return pop up in Paris
items.summary_auto: A social media post by Dion herself and the new posters across Paris are fueling speculation about the singer's reported return to performing. But representatives for the Quebec star have not confirmed any performances.

items.title: Arrest made in hit and run that killed Ottawa school crossing guard
items.summary_auto: Ottawa police have arrested a 19-year-old man after a school crossing guard died following a hit-and-run collision near a Barrhaven elementary school on Monday.

items.title: Edmonton emergency doctors and hospitalists warn of ER backlogs when stipend payments end
items.summary_auto: Some family doctors and emergency room physicians working in Edmonton hospitals warn that an April 1 end to stipend pay arrangements could put patient care at risk and increase suffering.

items.title: Some U.S. car buyers envy what they cannot have — affordable Chinese EVs
items.summary_auto: Survey shows 40 per cent of U.S. consumers ​support Chinese auto imports, while political opposition in U.S. remains strong

items.title: U.S. to pay French company $1B to scrap offshore wind projects
items.summary_auto: The Trump administration will pay $1 billion to a France's TotalEnergies to walk away from two U.S. offshore wind leases as the administration ramps up its campaign against offshore wind and other renewable energy.

items.title: Trout catch at Kejimkujik decimated by invasion of voracious predator
items.summary_auto: Kejimkujik National Park is pausing its annual brook trout census because an invasive species is devastating the local ecosystem.

items.title: Moms feel the burn — and a sense of community — while their babies are looked after
items.summary_auto: The Islander Athletics "mom club" allows parents to work out while their kids are looked after, building muscle and community.

items.title: When a beloved pizza joint got bombarded with 1-star Google reviews, a London, Ont., neighbourhood fought back
items.summary_auto: When the owner of a Byron pizzeria got hit with negative comments in a suspected "review bomb" members of the community countered with a review bomb of their own, this time with positive feedback.

items.title: How RCMP spies infiltrated the 1970s Indigenous rights movement
items.summary_auto: A CBC Indigenous investigation has found the Cold War-era RCMP Security Service had a widespread and intrusive surveillance operation targeting at least 30 legitimate Indigenous political organizations and hundreds of Indigenous people.

items.title: Ontario mandated e-learning to expand course options. Some worry it's being used to boost marks
items.summary_auto: Six years after the Ontario government made e-learning a mandatory requirement for a high school diploma, students don't appear to be taking online school to diversify their course selection as the province had intended, according to a CBC News analysis of provincial enrolment data.

items.title: Are Iranian 'sleeper cells' a threat to Canadians? Here’s what intelligence experts say
items.summary_auto: Rhetoric around Iranian sleeper cells has spiked since the onset of the U.S.-Israel war with Iran. Counter-intelligence experts and a former spy tell CBC News that while the regime may not use dormant agents in Canada, the threats of lone wolf attacks and 'criminal proxies' are real.

items.title: Syrians fleeing Israeli strikes in Lebanon return home to a country still rebuilding
items.summary_auto: More than 125,000 people have crossed from Lebanon into Syria this month, fleeing escalating violence and returning to a country that's still struggling to rebuild after more than a decade of civil war.

items.title: Smith-Carney pipeline deal to miss early deadlines, premier says
items.summary_auto: The first set of deadlines included in the energy and climate deal struck between Prime Minister Mark Carney and Alberta Premier Danielle Smith are not expected to be met, showing early challenges with the agreement that includes pursuing a new export pipeline from Alberta to the West Coast.
